meaning: adj.稀少的,很少发生的,罕见的
examples: Muggings are relatively infrequent in this area.
Kids and teens who do read frequently, compared to infrequent readers, have more books in the home.
He moved on, aimlessly, and in a little while the houses thinned, and the passers-by were infrequent.
Professor David Chadwick and colleagues recruited about 1, 400 people with single or infrequent seizures.
The taxpayer has had infrequent contact with the account and has exercised negligible control over it.
